entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
entry.content:
@MKreitlein : Great questions, however intention is to keep the limited as FAQs and not the technical details.

One question regarding No. 12, embedded SAC: I'm not sure where I read or heard it, but I think I understood that the embedded SAC in S/4 HANA Public Cloud would disappear sooner or later?

=>Please refer: SAP Analytics Cloud, embedded edition – the Way Ahead

And my most anticipated question about the Foundation services, is unfortunately still not answered satisfactory 🤔 : Wow exactly will SAP handle the Data Delta Extraction technically, from S4 CDS Views into the Data Lake Files?

=>Upon activation of a Data Product, the Foundation Service (FOS) layer initiates the data replication process. This involves two key steps: 1) The Foundation Service triggers the replication. 2) On the S/4HANA PCE side, the ABAP Data Integration components, specifically the ABAP Push Replication Service (APRS), replicate data from released CDS Views to SAP Business Data Cloud. This replication includes pushing metadata (CSN, ORD), followed by an initial data load, and then ongoing delta updates.

Refer SAP Note: 3500131 - ABAP Data Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud

Will there be lots of Notes or a huge TCI be required to be implemented in S4 or will it be the same technology like today when e.g. using Replication Flows into SAP Datasphere? It would be really helpful if we could see a picture/slide about those technical details soon 🤗

Yes, same like Replication flow scenario. Refer SAP Note: 3500131 - ABAP Data Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud

Happy Learning, Jeet

entry.title: Use a Datasphere View in a SAP CAP service over an HDI container - View not exposed for consumption
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am trying to interact with a Datasphere View from a SAP CAP service. I am following the mission as follows:

https://github.com/SAP-samples/datasphere-build-apps-data-entry

The issue is when I am trying to deploy the SAP CAP service (https://github.com/SAP-samples/datasphere-build-apps-data-entry/tree/main/documentation/develop/deploy-cap-application) as I am getting the error as follows:

error: "SAC$TEC"."HDI_GRANTOR_FOR_CUPS": line 43 col 9 (at pos 2079): Unsupported Feature: Cannot grant the requested privilege, because SAC.ZGV_SO_ICOMMNETS is not exposed for consumption)

This is at high level what we have:

A view created in Datasphere which is exposed for consumption.

 

A technical user with privileges to read and write.

A in BTP a User Provided Service named "datasphere-sac-space-ups" with the details of the above user:

 

{
    "password": "XXXXXXXXX",
    "procedure": "HDI_GRANTOR_FOR_CUPS",
    "procedure_schema": "SAC$TEC",
    "schema": "SAC",
    "tags": [
        "hana"
    ],
    "type": "procedure",
    "user": "SAC#SAC_POC"
}

 

.hdbgrants as follows:

{
	"datasphere-sac-space-ups": {
		"object_owner": {
			"roles": [],
			"container_roles": [],
			"object_privileges": [
                {
				  "type": "VIEW",
				  "name": "ZGV_SO_ICOMMNETS",
				  "privileges_with_grant_option": [
				    "SELECT","READ","WRITE"
				  ]
				}
			]
		}
	}
}

.hdsynonym

{
  "SAP_POC": {
    "target": {
        "schema": "SAC",        
        "object": "ZGV_SO_ICOMMNETS"
    }
  }
}

But unfortunately, we are getting the error already mentioned when deploying the CAP application.

error: "SAC$TEC"."HDI_GRANTOR_FOR_CUPS": line 43 col 9 (at pos 2079): Unsupported Feature: Cannot grant the requested privilege, because SAC.ZGV_SO_ICOMMNETS is not exposed for consumption)

Any ideas about what may be happening?

Thanks in advance!

C.

entry.title: Best Practices for Onboarding and MiniEC – With a Focus on Offboarding and Crossboarding
entry.content:
In this blog, the product management @emily_nagengast @Jayananda_Kotri  @smitajalit , product advisor @Shelly_Allen , partner management @PaulT , and knowledge expert team (I, @BarbaraSerio, @emicoke, @CarolinaCLeite, @Kanika_Khullar) would like to share some insights about onboarding with MiniEC and SAP on-prem as an external HRIS system.

What is Employee Central Mini-Master? 

Since Onboarding is built on the same platform as Employee Central it uses the same structure and data model. To maximize the benefits of Onboarding, it’s ideal for Employee Central to be already in place or implemented simultaneously. However in cases where the customer has not licensed Employee Central, a limited version of Employee Central - called EC Mini-Master needs to be used. EC Mini-Master provides the necessary structure and data model, but does not provide the same level of features as full Employee Central. Additionally, it’s important to understand that not all Onboarding features will be available. There are also configuration and setup considerations to keep in mind.

EC Mini-Master comes at no additional cost to the customer. When using EC Mini-Master its necessary to also differentiate between Full EC licensed users and a Functional EC licensed user. 
Typically a full EC licensed user would be an employee of the company, while a functional users would be external and not employed by the company. There are some other scenarios, and a full description can be found on the following help portal guide. Managing User License Compliance  

Below are the two user type scenarios and we will examine both in detail.

  • EC Mini-Master with functional users
  • EC Mini-Master with full EC licenses but full EC not yet implemented

EC Mini-Master with Functional Users

In this scenario, it's not possible to change employee data because no full licenses are available for Employee Central. For that reason, Crossboarding and Offboarding cannot be fully covered. However, there are no technical constraints to triggering those flows. The rules used in managing business configuration will be triggered even if the termination event, for example, is added from SAP on-prem with integration.
Please review the IDP (Implementation Design Principle) document below on custom integration.

SuccessFactors Integrations: Best Practices using SAP SuccessFactors APIs for Custom Integrations

If the employee, manager, and HR team work only on the tasks in the programs and no data reviews are triggered, offboarding and crossboarding processes can be configured.

Note: Restarting onboarding is not supported when using EC Mini-Master with functional users.

Configuration steps/actions:

  1. SAP on-prem mapping configuration for replication of employee data on SF
  2. Configuration of business rule on SF EC to trigger offboarding
  3. Terminate employee in SAP on-prem
  4. Run replication report on SAP on-prem
  5. Intelligent service triggered and offboardee landed in the dashboard

 

 

Employee Data Replication

Employee Data replication, as per Best Practice, must be implemented using Business Integration Builder (BIB). BIB framework includes configuration layer, reports for triggering Organizational and Employees Data replication on backend side, it takes advantage of prepackaged Integration Flows on BTP Integration Suite and of the SuccessFactors oData API to upsert data into Employee Central entities (that means Crossboarding and Offboarding processes can be triggered from every external tool using SuccessFactors oData APIs for updating employees data).

BIB configuration documentation for replicating Employee and Organizational Data from SAP ERP to Employee Central can be found at following link: 

Introduction to Employee Data Replication to Employee Central

EC Mini-Master with Full EC Licenses, Pending Full EC Implementation

In this scenario, employee data can potentially be changed. Therefore, full offboarding and crossboarding processes can be covered. Below are some do's and don'ts for this scenario.

Leading practice recommends having one system as the source of changes for employee data. We recommend leveraging employee events in SAP on-prem and then updating EC. We don't recommend using hybrid scenarios (some events generated in EC and others in SAP on-prem) just because the customer has full licenses of EC. This hybrid scenario can generate inconsistencies and require many custom integrations. However, it is possible to start the employee data review by asking, for example, the offboardee to change their email to a personal one. Again, you need to ensure that the information is updated in SAP on-prem to avoid inconsistencies.

In addition to the information shared in the blog above, we would encourage our partners to view the following document which outlines for configuration partners some of the additional considerations when using EC Mini-Master and how this impacts both Onboarding and additional processes. 
A partner S-user is required to access this document - Onboarding with EC Mini Master Scoping and Considerations

Summary

Ideally, we recommend that all customers have an Employee Central license, as it offers the best user experience—not just during onboarding but as part of a comprehensive HR system. However, we hope the information above is helpful if you encounter customer scenarios where this isn’t the case. If you have any further questions, please post them below and tag the authors.

entry.title: Free SAP BTP own Global Account
entry.content:
First, need to create your global account using the "Pay-As-You-Go for SAP BTP" option, on the SAP BTP page. 

https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/pricing.html

After clicking "Start Now", you need to Sign in using your SAP ID.

Pay-As-You-Go for SAP BTP

Add SAP BTP Pay-AS-You-Go into the cart and finalize the buying process. All the way will show a $0 (Zero) amount to pay.

After finalizing the purchase, SAP will send an email like this one on the left and contract approved on the right:

Congratulations! You have your own SAP BTP account! Now the SAP system will perform the provisioning... It takes sometime. Be patient, You will receive another email like this one:

In that email shoeing the provisioning, you can access your SAP BTP instance following the link in the section "System URL", like https://emea.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap/cockpit/#/globalaccount/ XXXXXXXXX

Done!!! the creation of global account is complete!

In the next article I will show how to create the sub-accounts and create instances of Hana, ABAP, BAS, etc...

entry.title: Re: Application Passcode for MDK Extension Can't Generate...
entry.content:
Mark,

Thank you for the clarification.  It helps a lot.

We introduced the MDK App Extension template for use with SaaS based applications like SAP Maintenance Assistant.  It currently does not apply to SAP Service and Asset Manager installations since they are non SaaS based subscription apps.   To create your Z project you should still select the MDK Project template type.

If you were connecting to a SaaS application the passcode feature should be already enabled to allow that to work.  Since you need to use the MDK Project template you should not run into this using that template type.

entry.title: Re: SAML and 2212795
entry.content: You have modified the URL that SAML was using by changing HTTP to HTTPS. Per KBA https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2791348 you must fix the HTTP URL replacing it with the HTTPS, and depending on the port (if not standard 80/443) it must also be modified for instance non standard port 8080 on tomcat gets changed to 8443. Then you have to redo the metadata and it should get everything back up.

entry.title: Re: App to App Seamless Logon
entry.content: When the user logs into the application the MDK client will display a webview (or CCT) and redirect to the Identity Provider to perform the actual login process. The MDK client does not directly handle the login. By default it uses a webview which is limited in what it can access. If you enable AllowCerts the login flow uses the CCT which can access the device certificates etc and may help. Once the client is logged in they are not prompted for credentials again until their OAuth token/refresh token expires. When launching the app again they will only be prompted for the passcode (assuming you have it enabled).

entry.title: Transforming Incentive Management with AI: Introducing Joule Integration
entry.content:
The complexity of modern sales compensation demands innovative solutions. Today, SPM is introducing Joule integration into Incentive Management, marking a strategic shift toward AI-enhanced compensation management. This integration represents the first phase of our comprehensive vision to transform how organizations handle sales compensation through artificial intelligence.

Enhancing the Sales Experience

In this initial release, we've focused on streamlining access to compensation information through natural language interactions. Sales representatives can now navigate their compensation details through intuitive conversations with Joule, our AI copilot. Whether checking payment summaries or reviewing commission details, the information is accessible through simple, natural language queries.

Key features include:

  • Conversational access to payment and commission information
  • Position-specific data retrieval
  • Period-based information access
  • Comprehensive transaction visibility

Information Categories

  • Payment details and summaries
  • Deposits and credits
  • Commission information
  • Transaction and order details
  • Measurement and incentive data
  • Balance and adjustment information

For example, sales representatives can ask:

  • "What are my payment details for August 2024?"
  • "What is my commission for the Sales Director position?"
  • "Show me my deposits for this month."
  • "What are my credit adjustments?"

Understanding complex commission calculations traditionally requires navigating multiple screens or consulting compensation specialists.

 

 

 



 

Part of a Broader Vision

This integration marks the beginning of a comprehensive transformation in Incentive Management. Our roadmap encompasses:

Intelligent Operations

  • AI-driven automation and insights
  • Proactive issue detection
  • Intelligent decision support

Enhanced Experience

  • Natural language interactions
  • Personalized insights
  • Self-service enablement

Data-Driven Strategy

  • Real-time performance analytics
  • Predictive modeling
  • Strategic insights

Adaptive Systems

  • Flexible rule management
  • Dynamic optimization
  • Continuous learning

Looking Ahead

Integrating Joule into Incentive Management represents more than just a feature addition—it's a fundamental shift in how organisations can interact with and manage their compensation programs. As we continue to develop and enhance these capabilities, we focus on delivering practical innovations that drive efficiency and value for our users.

Early Adopter Care Program: Joule in SAP Incentive Management

Join

  • Customer participation will be confirmed upon review/approval of the project scope completed in the following online form.* 

Key Data

  • Registration Start:* March 03, 2025
  • Release to Customer:* March 13, 2025
  • Registration End:* March 28, 2025

Conditions for Participation

Participation in the Early Adopter Care program is voluntary, at the sole discretion of SAP, and dependent on the following prerequisites:

We are looking for existing SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management customers in Germany and the United States who:

  • Have an active production environment with at least 50 sales representatives in hyperscalers
  • Actively use My Incentive Dashboard  
  • Plan to use Joule in production and are willing to take the necessary steps for integration
  • Are eager to become a reference to promote incremental usage and adoption
  • Are aware of the complete set of restrictions, which can be found here: General Constraints for Joule
  • Have stable transaction volumes (minimum 1000 transactions per month)
  • Have multiple compensation plan types in use
  • Can commit to regular feedback sessions

During this EAC program, Joule in SAP Incentive Management is available free of charge

Feedback

We aim to improve our solutions to meet our customers' needs continuously. We invite you to share your experience with us through:

  • Bi-weekly feedback sessions
  • Online surveys
  • Usage analytics
  • Feature validation workshops

Support

As a participant in the Early Adopter Care Program, you will benefit from:

  • Dedicated support by the Early Adopter Care Project Coach
  • Direct access to the development organization
  • Priority issue resolution
  • Implementation guidance
  • Regular check-in sessions

Disclaimers

  • Future developments are at SAP's discretion and may be changed at any time without notice for any reason.
  • Dates are subject to change.
  • Please use the Chrome browser if you experience issues with this site or the associated links.
  • All data processing follows standard SAP security and privacy guidelines.

entry.title: SAP IBP Configuration - Configure Weighted Average in SAP IBP
entry.content:
The IBP_WEIGHTEDAVG function in SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) is used to calculate a weighted average for key figures. This function is particularly useful when you need to aggregate data in a way that accounts for the relative importance or weight of different data points.

How It Works:

  • Input Key Figures: You provide the key figures that need to be averaged.
  • Weights: You specify the weights that determine the relative importance of each data point.
  • Calculation: The function calculates the weighted average by multiplying each data point by its corresponding weight, summing these products, and then dividing by the sum of the weights.

Example:

If you have sales data for different regions and want to calculate a weighted average sales price, you can use the IBP_WEIGHTEDAVG function to ensure that regions with higher sales volumes have a greater impact on the average price.

Benefits:

  • Accuracy: Provides a more accurate representation of data by considering the relative importance of different data points.
  • Flexibility: Can be used for various types of key figures, such as prices, costs, or quantities.

In this article, we will create weighed price key figure, using the simplified calculation and using the historical sales as a weight.

Create additional configuration for the key figure FORECASTPRICE

1. Create another calculation definition for the key figure called FORECASTPRICE at the PERPRODCUST planning level.
Use the following data:

2. Add the following calculation: FORECASTPRICE@PERPRODCUST = SUM("FORECASTPRICE@PERPRODCUSTCURR")

3. Create a key figure called FORECASTPRICECALCWEIGHTED, which will calculate a weighted price for a customer, using actual sales as a weight.
Use the following data and write the calculation.

FieldValue
Key Figure IDFORECASTPRICECALCWEIGHTED
Base Planning LevelPERPRODCUST
CalculatedX

FORECASTPRICECALCWEIGHED @request = IBP_WEIGHTEDAVG("FORECASTPRICE@PERPRODCUST","ACTUALSQTY@PERPRODCUST",''CALCULATEDNUMERATOR'')

 

4. Activate your planning area.

 

Test Your Solution Using Planner Workspace

1. Test your solution by creating a Planner Workspace using the following information.

FieldValue
Time: 
Time PeriodMonthly
From Current Month
ToCurrent Month + 12
RollingYes
Attributes: 
Product IDSelected
Customer IDSelected
Key Figures: 
Actuals QtySelected
Forecast PriceSelected
Forecast Price(calculated weighted)Selected
Currency ToUSD
UOM To EA
Filters 
Customer ID104

entry.title: Dynamic Interface on Cloud Integration - part 5
entry.content:
I’ll break this article into:

  • Problem/Context 
  • Our approach 
  • Git into Partner Directory 
  • Generic iflow 
  • Automated unit testing (this link)
  • Limitations and Future plans

One great advantage of relying on git is that you can have the freedom to organize your git repository however you want. We thought that we usually struggle on regular interface development to have sample data to test our interfaces. What if we could stored these test data on git as part of our scenario and unit test it at the end of the day?

This is exactly what we've done!

Let's create a folder named Sample at same level as the "Preparation Steps" folder.

Then inside of the Sample folder we'll have key pair files of REQ (requests) and their respective RSP (responses)

This is a great way to test for regressions since our Jenkins pipeline is prepared to check for each of the dynamic interfaces deployed if there is a 'Samples' folder. If so, it calls the dynamic interface with each of the REQ files and asserts a match on the response to the respective RSP associated file. Whenever running via test automation, we skip the sending to any target. It's just Unit testing of all payload transformations.

Now let's try to manually test one of these files just to see it working. We can test requirement 3 which was one of the most complex that we've defined. Example of input

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<root>
	<process>CreateBatch</process>
	<region>EMEA</region>
	<prod_date>20240730</prod_date> <!-- format of dates in S4 is yyyymmdd -->
	<quantity>1000</quantity>
	<batch>1</batch>
	<batchplant>CH01</batchplant>
	<material>12345678901</material>
	<country>PT</country>
	<!-- other fields not relevant for this demo -->
</root>

 

What shall we expect?

It's a CreateBatch process so we shall format the date into yyyy-MM-dd, its EMEA region so the quantity shall be formatted. Finally country is PT therefore quantity shall be doubled. From target perspective, final url should be https://testnupeemeabatch.free.beeceptor.com

And it worked! 

In this chapter we covered how hard it is to find sample payload files for our tests and how cool would it be to test them automatically whenever they exist. On the final part we'll look into current limitations and future plans for this dynamic interface

entry.title: Generation of MPIDs for the SAP ABAP system
entry.content:
Hello, everyone.

In this article, we will discuss the Generation of MPID for the SAP ABAP system.

Get system details such as installed product versions, components, and database version.

SAP Support Portal Home –>Maintenance –> Maintenance Planner –> Access Maintenance Planner –>Explore Systems–>Enter the SID in search bar–>double click on the SID–>click on the center of the circle (SID).

Click on the plan

Check the radio button update SAP NetWeaver for NW SPS updates.

Select the required SPS and click on the confirm selection button.

Please ensure that the selected SPS has been updated in the Target software information.

In our case SAP NetWeaver 7.4 SPS 32

Similarly, repeat the process for EHP3 for SAP SCM 7.0 SPS-Update.

Select the radio button of Install or maintain an Enhancement Package –>EHP3 for SAP SCM 7.0 –> Required SPS–>Click on the confirm selection

Please ensure that the selected SPS has been updated in the Target software information.

In our case EHP3 for SAP SCM 7.0 SPS 29.
SAP SCM 7.0 SPS 26 is inclusive of EHP3 for SAP SCM 7.0 SPS 29

Click on the next button.

In our case BI_CONT version is 747.

Check the SAP NW 7.31 BI content addon 747

Details of OS/DB files –>Select OS (In our case Windows) –>

Check the following and click on the confirm selection
SAP-ASE (Internally contains DB dependent kernels)
SAP-Hostagent 7.22
SAP IGS 7.53/7.54 (Depends on Kernel)
Software Update Manager (Depends on ABAP/JAVA)

For IGS Version, please follow the 1491848 - What IGS should I be using with my Web AS? - SAP for Me

Verify once again by dropping down the OS_DB, ABAP, NON-ABAP and Stack independent files.
If any component patch level is not required, you can un-Check that component and click on the next

For Stack XML file: click on the Download stack xml

For SPS update Plan document: Drop-down additional downloads –> Download PDF

Click on the push to download basket (all required s/w components will be added in to basket with date and time stamp)


Thanks,
Mani

entry.title: ABAP Code Optimization Techniques in 7.50+
entry.content:
Introduction

Optimizing ABAP code is essential for ensuring efficient SAP applications. Poorly optimized code can lead to high memory consumption, increased execution time, and system slowdowns. This guide covers key ABAP 7.50+ optimization techniques that enhance performance while maintaining readability and maintainability.

  1. Optimize Database Access

     a. Efficient SELECT Queries

  • Avoid SELECT *: Retrieve only necessary columns to reduce memory usage.
SELECT FROM mara FIELDS matnr,
                        maktx INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_mara)
                  WHERE matnr = '100000'.
  • Use SELECT INTO TABLE instead of looping with SELECT:
SELECT FROM mara FIELDS matnr,
                        maktx INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_mara)
                  WHERE matnr IN @LT_matnr.
  • ABAP 7.50+ allows the use of CASE statements inside SELECT queries to simplify conditional logic:
SELECT matnr,
       CASE WHEN mtart = 'FERT' THEN 'Finished Product'
            WHEN mtart = 'HALB' THEN 'Semi-Finished Product'
            ELSE 'Other'
       END AS material_type
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_materials)
  FROM mara.
  • Using CORRESPONDING for Structure Mapping
DATA(lt_new_mara) = VALUE #( FOR wa IN lt_mara ( CORRESPONDING #( wa ) ) ).
  • Using VALUE Instead of INTO TABLE
DATA(lt_mara) = VALUE #( FOR wa IN lt_matnr WHERE ( matnr = wa-matnr )
                                                  ( matnr = wa-matnr,
                                                    maktx = wa-maktx ) ).

     b. Use Joins Instead of Nested SELECTs

Nested SELECTs increase database calls and degrade performance. Instead, use INNER JOIN:

SELECT mara~matnr, maktx, mtart
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_data)
  FROM mara
  INNER JOIN t134 ON mara~mtart = t134~mtart
  WHERE mara~matnr IN @LT_matnr.

     c. Use FOR ALL ENTRIES Instead of Loops with SELECT

  • Ensure lt_matnr is not empty to avoid fetching the entire table:
IF lt_matnr IS NOT INITIAL.
  SELECT matnr, maktx FROM mara
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_mara)
    WHERE matnr IN @LT_matnr.
ENDIF.

     2.Optimize Internal Table Operations

     a. Use Sorted and Hashed Tables for Faster Lookup

  • Sorted tables: Use when frequent reads with binary search are required.
  • Hashed tables: Best for key-based lookups. 
DATA(lt_mara) = VALUE SORTED TABLE OF mara WITH UNIQUE KEY matnr.

      b. Use READ TABLE with Binary Search

For standard tables, sort them before searching:

SORT lt_mara BY matnr.
READ TABLE lt_mara INTO DATA(ls_mara)
             WITH KEY matnr = '100000' BINARY SEARCH.

New Syntax for Filtering Instead of READ TABLE:

In new read syntax there is only implicit binary search when the internal table will be sorted internal table. 

DATA(lt_filtered) = FILTER #( lt_mara WHERE matnr = '10000001' ).

     3. Optimize Loop Performance

      a. Avoid Nested Loops

Instead of looping inside loops, use internal table joins where possible:

LOOP AT lt_mat INTO DATA(ls_mat).
  DATA(ls_mara) = FILTER #( lt_mara WHERE matnr = ls_mat-matnr ).
ENDLOOP.

       b. Use Field Symbols Instead of Work Areas

Using FIELD-SYMBOLS avoids unnecessary copies and improves performance:

LOOP AT lt_mara ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_mara>).
  <fs_mara>-maktx = 'Updated Value'.
ENDLOOP.

     4. Optimize String Operations

      a. Use String Templates Instead of Concatenation

String templates simplify concatenation and improve readability:

DATA(lv_text) = |Material { lv_matnr } belongs to { lv_mtart } category|.

       b. Use Built-in String Functions

Avoid manual looping for string modifications. Use built-in functions like

CONDENSE:

lv_string = CONDENSE( lv_string NO-GAPS ).

     5. Optimize Memory Usage

     a. Free Unused Internal Tables

After processing, free memory occupied by large internal tables:

FREE lt_mara.

      b. Use CLEAR Instead of REFRESH for Small Tables

CLEAR is better for small tables, while REFRESH only clears entries without freeing memory:

CLEAR lt_mara.

Conclusion

Efficient ABAP coding is crucial for SAP system performance. By optimizing database access, internal table operations, loops, and memory usage, you can significantly enhance execution speed and reduce system load.

Performance Analysis Tools:

  • SAT (Runtime Analysis)
  • ST05 (SQL Trace)
  • SE30 (Performance Trace)

Using these techniques ensures that your ABAP programs run efficiently, consume fewer resources, and deliver better performance.

entry.title: RAP Events
entry.content:
SAP RAP (ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model) supports events to handle business logic at different points in an application’s lifecycle. These events help in executing specific actions automatically or on-demand.  

  1. Types of Events in RAP 
  2. Implicit Events (Triggered Automatically) 

These are predefined events triggered by the RAP framework during different phases of the transaction.  

      Before-Save Event  

  • Occurs before the transaction is committed.  
  • Used for validations, automatic field updates, or derivations.  
  • Example: Auto-filling a timestamp before saving a record. 

       After-Save Event  

  • Occurs after the transaction is committed.  
  • Used for notifications, logging, or triggering workflows.  
  • Example: Sending an email after a purchase order is approved. 

 

     Explicit Events (Triggered Manually) 

These are custom events defined in the RAP behavior definition and triggered explicitly in the business logic.  

  • Definition: Declared in the behavior definition file (. behavior).  
  • Triggering: Raised in an ABAP class using RAISE EVENT.  
  • Consumption: Handled event handlers inside RAP behavior implementation classes. 

 Defining & Using RAP Events – Example 

Step 1: Define an Event in Behavior Definition 

Create an explicit event inside the RAP behavior definition (.behavior).  

 Step 2: Trigger the Event in the Behavior Implementation Class 

Raise the event inside an implementation class.  

Step 3: Handle the Event in Event Handler 

Define an event handler to execute logic when the event is raised.  

Use Cases of RAP Events 

Event Type 

Use Case Example 

Before-Save 

Validate input data before saving a sales order. 

After-Save 

Trigger a workflow after a new employee record is created. 

Explicit Event 

Notify external systems when an invoice is finalized. 

                                         

Scenario. 

Scenario: Automatic Order Confirmation Based on Stock Availability.   

Business Logic 

- When a Sales Order (SO) is created, a BEFORE SAVE event checks stock levels.   

- If stock is available, the SO is automatically confirmed (`CONFIRMED`) .   

- If stock is not available, the SO remains pending (`PENDING`) until replenishment.   

 Sales Table. 

 

@EndUserText.label : 'sales'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table znp_dt_sales {

  key client       : abap.clnt not null;
  key salesorderid : abap.numc(10) not null;
  itemid           : abap.numc(5);
  materialid       : abap.char(18);
  quantity         : abap.dec(13,3);
  currencycode     : abap.cuky;
  totalamount      : abap.dec(15,2);
  status           : abap.char(10);
  created_by       : abp_creation_user;
  created_at       : abp_creation_tstmpl;
  last_changed_by  : abp_locinst_lastchange_user;
  last_changed_at  : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;

}

 

Stocks table.

 

@EndUserText.label : 'stocks'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table znp_dt_stock {

  key client      : abap.clnt not null;
  key materialid  : abap.char(18) not null;
  plant           : abap.char(4) not null;
  stock           : abap.dec(13,3) not null;
  uom             : abap.unit(3) not null;
  last_updated_by : abap.sstring(12) not null;
  last_updated_on : abap.dats not null;

}

 

CDS  View Entity.

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@EndUserText.label: '###GENERATED Core Data Service Entity'
define root view entity ZR_NP_DT_SALES
  as select from ZNP_DT_SALES
{
  key salesorderid as Salesorderid,
  itemid as Itemid,
  materialid as Materialid,
  quantity as Quantity,
  @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [ {
    entity.name: 'I_CurrencyStdVH', 
    entity.element: 'Currency', 
    useForValidation: true
  } ]
  currencycode as Currencycode,
  totalamount as Totalamount,
  status as Status,
  @Semantics.user.createdBy: true
  created_by as CreatedBy,
  @Semantics.systemDateTime.createdAt: true
  created_at as CreatedAt,
  @Semantics.user.localInstanceLastChangedBy: true
  last_changed_by as LastChangedBy,
  @Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true
  last_changed_at as LastChangedAt
  
}

 

Abstract entity For Define the parameters of event.

 

@EndUserText.label: 'abstract ntity'
define abstract entity znp_s_eventsc
 
{
   Message : abap.char( 50 );
}

 

Behavior Defintion.

 

managed implementation in class ZBP_R_NP_DT_SALES unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;
define behavior for ZR_NP_DT_SALES alias ZrNpDtSales
persistent table znp_dt_sales
draft table znp_dt_sales_d
etag master LastChangedAt
early numbering
lock master total etag LastChangedAt
authorization master ( global )
with additional save

{


  field ( readonly )
  CreatedBy,
  CreatedAt,
  LastChangedBy,
  Salesorderid,
  Status,
  LastChangedAt;


 action confirm_order parameter znp_s_eventsc;
  event C_order parameter znp_s_eventsc;
  create;
  update;
  delete;

  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Discard;
  draft action Edit;
  draft action Resume;
  draft determine action Prepare;

  mapping for znp_dt_sales
    {
      Salesorderid  = salesorderid;
      Itemid        = itemid;
      Materialid    = materialid;
      Quantity      = quantity;
      Currencycode  = currencycode;
      Totalamount   = totalamount;
      Status        = status;
      CreatedBy     = created_by;
      CreatedAt     = created_at;
      LastChangedBy = last_changed_by;
      LastChangedAt = last_changed_at;
    }
}

 

 

In the class save_modified method raise the event if stock is not available,it will\

give message to user materials is out of stock.

 

 

IF create-zrnpdtsales IS NOT INITIAL .

      DATA ls_tab TYPE znp_dt_sales.
      DATA(ls_c) = VALUE #(   lt_c[ 1 ] OPTIONAL ) .
*

      READ ENTITIES OF zr_np_dt_sales IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY ZrNpDtSales
      ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #( (   %key = ls_c-%key ) )
      RESULT DATA(lt_sales).

      DATA(ls_sales) = lt_sales[ 1 ].

      SELECT SINGLE FROM znp_dt_stock FIELDS stock
      WHERE materialid = _sales-Materialid
      INTO (lv_stock).


      IF ls_sales-Quantity > lv_stock.

        RAISE ENTITY EVENT zr_np_dt_sales~C_order
        FROM VALUE #( ( %key   = ls_u-%key
         %param = VALUE #( Message =
         'Sales order is Pending Due to low stock' ) ) ).
endif.

 

 

In  the event handler It will give the message.

 

CLASS lcl_local_events DEFINITION INHERITING FROM
cl_abap_behavior_event_handler .
  PRIVATE SECTION.
 METHODS confirm_order
 FOR ENTITY EVENT it_parameters FOR ZrNpDtSales~C_order.

Endclass.


Method  confirm_order.
data(ls_param) =  value #( it_paramter[ 1 ] optional ).
APPEND VALUE #( %tky = ls_sales-%tky
 %msg = me->new_message_with_text(
 severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
 text     = ls_param-message
 ) ) TO reported-zrnpdtsales.

 

while creating order if the if the quantity is more than the stocks it will give message

that stocks is not available, status is set to pending.

entry.title: Harnessing Excel for Enhanced Financial Data Management
entry.content:
In this age of digital and technological advancements in all areas, the need for analyzing and understanding data is very important for professionals across all industries. As an SAP enthusiast with interest in data analysis and management, I wanted to know more about data analysis and integration for better understanding of data, this is why I started learning more about the most used tool for data analysis – Microsoft Excel. I made it a point to explore all capabilities of Excel and data management. Hence, I created a mock financial dataset that would serve as a medium to test out my excel skills and also to explore how Excel’s functionalities could complement and enhance the data management processes in SAP.

 

For any kind of personal finance tracking, the three most important elements are: - Income, Expense and Savings. I made separate tables for the same. For any inconsistencies with my Data, I used Excel’s ‘Find and Replace’ functionality. To ensure there were no discrepancies and to have uniformity throughout the data, I used ‘Data Validation’.For understanding and using the analytical capabilities of Excel, I used the Pivot Tables feature of Excel. These tools allowed me to visualize and summarize the financial data effectively, enabling the calculation of totals and identification of trends with just a few clicks. To further enhance the understanding of the data, I used various chart types, transforming the numerical information into engaging visual cues. My efforts led to the creation of a comprehensive dashboard. To make that, I strategically arranged the charts and tables into a single worksheet. I understood my financial health better with this dashboard. Conditional formatting helped me to locate where my expenses were more and where it was okay, it really helped me for quick identification of these areas. Excel’s inbuilt formulas like ‘SUM’ and ‘AVERAGE’ were instrumental to perform essential calculations, thus, making the analytical processes easier.

 

The project has been more than an exercise, a major learning to be proficient in Excel. The skills that I gained and applied throughout this project are not only valuable for Excel environments but also highly transferrable to SAP environments as well. I gained the ability to manage data effectively and make it more insightful with this experience. Learning data analysis in Excel is crucial as it's a universally accessible tool that simplifies the handling and interpretation of data. It's beneficial for professionals who need to quickly analyze and present data in a clear manner. My experience with Excel highlights its practicality and the advantages it offers in various scenarios, making it an essential skill for anyone looking to enhance their data management capabilities. The accompanying screenshots serve as visual guide through each phase of the process, from initial data entry to the polished final dashboard. They showcased the transformative power of Excel.

entry.title: How do you model two production resources for the same product id in IBP?
entry.content: I have two resources that are capable of producing the same items. In Production Source Resource I have the two resources entered under the same SourceID (not sure if this is correct?). When I run the heuristic it's taking capacity off of both resources for this item, but it's not accounting for total capacity between the two resources. Ideally it would use a designated resource first and then when that one is full switch over to the other resource, but I was hoping at a minimum it would at least add the capacity of both resources together so when it allocates production it's pulling the correct capacity utilization.

This is the sample program, 

 

 

CALL FUNCTION 'ZTEST_FM_IN_BG' IN BACKGROUND TASK
  EXPORTING
    var1    = 15
    var2    = 3

  TABLES

lw_table-res = var1 + var2.

How to use the TRFC_GET_RETURN_DATA can I get the value of lt_table_main using this FM ?

entry.title: S/4Hana Brownfield Conversion Project
entry.content:
First, the system needs to be prepared, that is, the ECC system is moved to the S4 HANA structure by technical consultants (BASIS-ABAP). For this, they upload the relevant sap notes to the system and create a sandbox system. All module consultants provide testing and controls in this test system, then if necessary, the 2nd sandbox system can be installed as a development system or directly to the QA system, then the live system will be switched.

Throughout the project you will hear the following terms very often SI Check List and SI items

SI: Simplification Item.

Technical consultants run Simplification Item Check Report and if there is an error or inconsistency, they assign it to the relevant module consultants.

All module consultants check their own inconsistencies and errors and make the necessary corrections.

go to se38 /SDF/RC_START_CHECK

You can check SI ıtem and sap notes in this report

SAP Readiness Check Cloud Application

ZIP files are created and uploaded to the SAP Readiness Check Cloud App. The data here can be read by consultants and verified before being uploaded to the application.

For more information on this part, see this video published by SAP

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/SAAP/SAP_Activate/RC_Version_3.mp4

After this technical transition, module consultants start working on the transaction codes, tables, structures that change with S4 Hana in the sandbox system.

The most important topics are as follows;

-CVI integration, also called BP Configuration

-Transfer of price and credit master and credit management and control of the changing structure

For example, additional processing is required for Condition Master Data mappings because the data will now be used in the PRCD_ELEMENTS table instead of the KONV table. For more detailed information you can take a look at this note

https://me.sap.com/notes/2267308

If there is a Rebate Process, the Settlement management- conditon contract structure that comes with S/4 Hana can be recommended or not configured

If there are licensed products such as DSD, Aatp, etc., their licenses will be renewed and system connections will be made so that they can be used in S/4. For more detailed information, you can take a look at these notes

Aatp

https://me.sap.com/notes/2267745

https://me.sap.com/notes/2209696

Rebate

https://me.sap.com/notes/2267377/E

Except for these

Some data model simplifications have been implemented for the SD field. Removed status tables VBUK-VBUP instead status fields have been moved to tables VBAK and VBAP for orders, LIKP and LIPS for deliveries, VBRK for billing documents for more details https://me.sap.com/notes/2267306

Instead of S066, S067 tables used in Credit Management, UKMBP_CMS_SGM & UKMBP_CMS tables came in S/4 Hana. For more details https://me.sap.com/notes/2270544

For Customer master data, KNA1, KNB1, KNVV, KNVP tables were used in ECC System. BP master data will be created in S/4 Hana, BUT000-BP: General data I BUT100-BP: Roles tables will be used.

Apart from these, there are CVI_LINK tables. Customer-BP and Vendor-BP information is kept in these tables. When the numbers do not match, it can check through these.

Customer-BP table V_CVI_CUST_LINK

BP and Supplier table V_CVI_VEND_LINK

I would be happy to read your thoughts and comments and if there are better ways to fulfil the requirement, please leave them in the comment section below.

 

Thanks so much

entry.title: SAP Change Document(CD): Special Scenarios with SOLMAN (Solution Manager)
entry.content:
SAP SOLMAN is a tool used by organizations to manage their SAP landscapes effectively. One of the great features of SOLMAN is its Change Request Management (ChaRM), which provides a structured approach to managing transport requests (TRs) in SAP.

Since many clients and customers are using SOLMAN, and it will be supported until 2027, this blog will explore two special scenarios that often arise in SOLMAN.


Scenario 1: Handling Released Transport Requests in SOLMAN (when changes are not moved to PROD)

In certain situations, you may need to segregate objects from a released transport request. Since decoupling a released TR is not permitted, follow these steps:

  • Create a New Change Document (CD): Create a new CD to manage the segregation process.
  • Segregate Objects from the Older CD: Identify and segregate the objects from the existing CD.
  • Lock Objects in a New TR of the New CD: Create a new transport request within the new CD and lock the segregated objects accordingly.
  • CSOL Lock Management: If you're unable to lock the objects, it might be due to an active CSOL (Cross-System Object Lock). Use transaction code /N/TMWFLOW/LOCKMON or Administration Cockpit - CSOL in SOLMAN to remove the CSOL lock, allowing you to proceed with locking the objects in the new TR.
  • Change the status of existing CD to WITHDRAWN: Forcefully change the status of an existing CD to "WITHDRAWN" using the report CRM_SOCM_SERVICE_REPORT.
    Note: You might encounter a ToC (Transport of Copies) document somewhere, so proceed diligently with above steps.

 

Scenario 2: Managing TRs after QA Refreshes (when changes are not moved to PROD)

A common challenge during QA refreshes is that only changes made till the production system may be reflected in the QA environment. To address this, you can:

  • Consolidate Changes: Instead of manually moving all the transport requests (TRs) of a change document (CD) one by one via STMS_IMPORT, include all objects from various TRs of the CD into a single TR. By doing this, all active versions of dependent objects will be locked in one TR. This approach is particularly helpful in implementation projects when you are building something from scratch.
  • Move Consolidated TR via SOLMAN: Use SOLMAN to transport this consolidated TR and validate that all necessary changes are visible in the QA system.

By following the above actions for specific scenarios, we can efficiently handle CDs in SOLMAN with ease.

These insights are based on my experience. I recommend connecting with your Basis/Solman team for further guidance. I would greatly appreciate any additional details or new techniques you might have to offer. Your comments and feedback are welcome!

entry.title: SG GST Report - Not able to download data from preview
entry.content:

However for Singapore, once the report has been run, we get the message "SG GST Report cannot be downloaded". In note 3451521 - Information "& cannot be downloaded" in app Run Statutory Reports they address this issue and the solution is to download the data from the Preview. But for Singapore there is no way to download the data from the preview (download button does not appear). Any ideas how to solve it?. Thank you.

entry.title: Content-Based or Non-Key Lookup in SAP Cloud Integration (SCI/CPI): Lookups Without Fixed Keys
entry.content:
Content-Based Lookup in SAP CPI Without Key-Value Pairs
In most SAP CPI integrations, we rely on key-based lookups using maps or content enrichers. However, there are scenarios where a key-value pair lookup is not feasible.

For instance, if the key involves an integer or double data type with a vast range, creating a mapping for every possible key-value pair becomes impractical. In such cases, we can use content-based search or iterative search to find the relevant matches dynamically.

Use Case: Matching a Person with a Group
Suppose we need to match a Person XML with a Group XML. A Person should be linked to a Group if their company, department, and quantity fields satisfy the given conditions. However, the quantity is a range of numbers, meaning that rather than looking for an exact match, we need to determine if the person's quantity falls within the specified range for a group. Once a match is found, the corresponding Group is attached to the Person Node.

My Approach:
Merge the Person and Group XML into a single structure using CPI functions. This allows us to process the data efficiently within a Groovy script.

Sample Input Payload:

 

<Messages>
  <Message1>
    <group>
      <group>
        <company>ABC</company>
        <department>MERCURY</department>
        <group>1</group>
        <contractMoreThan>0</contractMoreThan>
        <contractLessThan>5</contractLessThan>
      </group>
      <group>
        <company>DEF</company>
        <department>VENUS</department>
        <group>2</group>
        <contractMoreThan>5</contractMoreThan>
        <contractLessThan>11</contractLessThan>
      </group>
      <group>
        <company>DEF</company>
        <department>EARTH</department>
        <group>3</group>
        <contractMoreThan>10</contractMoreThan>
        <contractLessThan>20</contractLessThan>
      </group>
      <group>
        <company>DEF</company>
        <department>MARS</department>
        <group>3</group>
        <contractMoreThan>10</contractMoreThan>
        <contractLessThan>20</contractLessThan>
      </group>
      <group>
        <company>GHI</company>
        <department>JUPITER</department>
        <group>4</group>
        <contractMoreThan>30</contractMoreThan>
        <contractLessThan>365</contractLessThan>
      </group>
    </group>
    <Person>
      <Person>
        <id>111</id>
        <company>DEF</company>
        <department>EARTH</department>
        <quantity>13</quantity>
      </Person>
      <Person>
        <id>222</id>
        <company>GHI</company>
        <department>JUPITER</department>
        <quantity>350</quantity>
      </Person>
    </Person>
  </Message1>
</Messages>

 

 

 

 

 

Groovy Script for Matching
The following Groovy script processes the XML by iterating through each Person node and finding the corresponding Group based on company, department, and quantity conditions.

 

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import groovy.xml.XmlUtil
import groovy.util.Node
import groovy.util.slurpersupport.NodeChild

def Message processData(Message message) {
    def body = message.getBody(String)
    def xml = new XmlSlurper().parseText(body)

    def root = new Node(null, 'root')

    xml.Message1.Person.Person.each { person ->
        def company = person.company.text()
        def department = person.department.text()
        def quantity = person.quantity.toDouble()

        println "company: $company"
        println "department: $department"
        println "quantity: $quantity"
        println "----"

        def personNode = new Node(null, 'Person')
        def clonedPerson = new Node(null, 'Person')

        person.children().each { child ->
            clonedPerson.append(new Node(null, child.name(), child.text()))
        }
        personNode.append(clonedPerson)

        def groups = xml.Message1.group.group
        def matchedGroup = groups.find { group ->
            def contractMoreThan = group.contractMoreThan.text().toDouble()
            def contractLessThan = group.contractLessThan.text().toDouble()
           
        ///**This is where we set the criteria for the match**//
            company == group.company.text() &&
            department == group.department.text() &&
            quantity > contractMoreThan &&
            quantity < contractLessThan
        }

        if (matchedGroup) {
            def matchedGroupNode = new Node(null, 'matchedGroup')
            matchedGroup.children().each { child ->
                matchedGroupNode.append(new Node(null, child.name(), child.text()))
            }
            personNode.append(matchedGroupNode)
        }

        root.append(personNode)
    }

    def outputXml = XmlUtil.serialize(root)
    message.setBody(outputXml)
    return message
}

 

 

 

 

Output: As you can see the script correctly matched the Group and Person

 

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<root>
   <Person>
      <Person>
         <id>111</id>
         <company>DEF</company>
         <department>EARTH</department>
         <quantity>13</quantity>
      </Person>
      <matchedGroup>
         <company>DEF</company>
         <department>EARTH</department>
         <group>3</group>
         <contractMoreThan>10</contractMoreThan>
         <contractLessThan>20</contractLessThan>
      </matchedGroup>
   </Person>
   <Person>
      <Person>
         <id>222</id>
         <company>GHI</company>
         <department>JUPITER</department>
         <quantity>350</quantity>
      </Person>
      <matchedGroup>
         <company>GHI</company>
         <department>JUPITER</department>
         <group>4</group>
         <contractMoreThan>30</contractMoreThan>
         <contractLessThan>365</contractLessThan>
      </matchedGroup>
   </Person>
</root>

 

 

 

 

Conclusion

When a key-value pair lookup is not feasible, a content-based search approach like this is useful. By iterating through the XML and applying filtering logic dynamically, we can handle cases where an exact match is not predefined.

Please let me know if there are errors in this article or if there is anything we can further improve!

entry.title: Sales Promotions & Sales Deals Configurations and Applying Multiple Deals to an Order
entry.content:
Before starting the customizations of Promotions & Sales Deals, we need to make sure that we have 2 condition types to use for it.

First one, Sales Deal Determining Condition Type.

Which has condition class “H”(Determining Sales Deal). And the Calculation Type should be “G” Formula.

This condition type will not directly affect the pricing. It will just help to find the promotions.

 

The other condition type is regular discount condition type.

 

Access Sequence

For Promotion Discount cond. Type, you can use material based fields. (In Order to use multiple promotions for the same customer/materials, acces type should be “A”.)

 

For Determining cond. Type, you can use customer based fields. Access Type “B” should be used.

 

 

 

Customizing Promotions & Sales Deals

SPRO Path

IMG > Sales and Distribution> Basic Functions> Pricing> Pricing Agreements

 

 

1. Define Promotions

 

You can either use the standart promotion or create new one.

 

 

You can make changes on Proposed dates and text determination fields. The other fields are not changeable.

 

 

 

2. Define Sales Deals

 

 

Click “New Entries”.

 

Now in addition to Proposed Dates, we have Control area on this screen.

 

 

 

2.1- Condition Type Group: Specifies a grouping of condition types and condition tables that will be used for the sales deals. We will configure on the next steps.

 

2.2- Determines if the condition records and the Sales Deal have the same validity dates.

If the condition records may have a different validity date from the agreement, select this field.

 

2.3- Determines if the Sales Deal should have a higher-level agreement such as Promotions.

 

3. Defining Condition Type Groups

              

               You can create new condition type group by clicking “New Entries”

 

After entering the condition type group name and definition, you need to select Condition Type group category as “A” which is Price Agreements. So incompatible condition types can not be assigned to this group. (Such as Rebate Condition types).

 

 

 

 

 

4. Assign Condition Types/Tables to Condition group

You can click “New Entries” button and assign both condition types and table numbers to condition type group accordingly.

 

5. Assign Condition Type Group to Sales Deal

After creating new sales deal, it will automatically be created on this table as well. You can enter your condition type group and save.

 

 

Customizations are done. Now we will create promotions and sales deals

 

Go to VB31, select the promotion type and enter.

 

 

Write the Sales Organization, Distribution Channel and Division and enter.

 

 

You can determine the validity period on this screen. Define the description and save.

 

Promotion 90000010 created.

 

 

Now we will create a Sales Deal.

Go to VB21.

Write the Sales Deal type and enter.

 

 

 

 

Define the Sales Deal description, validity period, currency and reference promotion number. Then click “Conditions” button.

 

 

 

The condition type pop-up screen will appear. Select one of them and create condition record.

 

Enter the customer and go back.

 

You will see the number of conditions increased. Now select the other condition type.

 

Now you can enter the material and the amount of discount. Then you can save.

 

 

 

 

 

When you create an Order for related customer and material, we can see the discount has been applied.

 

 

 

On the pricing analysis, we can see the sales deal number.

 

 

On VBAP table, we can see the sales deal and promotion numbers.

KNUMA_PI : Promotion

KNUMA_AG: Sales Deal

 

 

 

On the item level of the invoice, we can see the promotion and sales deal number.

 

 

 

If you would like to apply multiple promotions to the same material.

You need to apply the requirement “62” to promotion discount condition type on the pricing procedure.

And the type of Access sequence of the condition type should be “A”.

 

 

Then multiple Sales Deals will be applied.

I hope these informations were useful. I would love to read your thoughts and comments.

Thank you 😊

entry.title: Delivery Split Structure
entry.content:
The logistics-related document created in the SAP system based on a specific customer order is called the sent delivery document. The delivery document includes the code, description, quantity, production location and warehouse location information of the products that need to be collected. While the warehouse manager collects these products, there may be a need to revise the quantity on the delivery document due to the reasons that the relevant product is not in the warehouse or the products do not fit in the incoming vehicle. In this case, a new delivery document must be created for the remaining amount in order to manage the shipment organization correctly.

In the standard infrastructure, the user can manually create a new delivery document by taking into account the specified process and the information in the delivery. Alternatively, the SAP system provides the opportunity to copy the information in the document and revise the quantity via the "VLSP" transaction code.

Configuration step:  SPRO-> Logistics execution -> Shipping -> Deliveries-> Subsequent Delivery Split

The "Split profile" is defined in the "Subsequent Delivery Split" configuration step.

The profile definition specifies how the parsed result document and the remaining document should behave.

Some details within the split profile are described as follows:

ResultInWMGroup: WM transfer of the delivery document resulting from split

InclRmdrInWMGrp: WM transfer of the delivery document remaining from split

Result in Shpmt: Including the delivery document resulting from split into the transportation

InclRmdrInShpmt: Including the delivery document remaining from split into the transportation

No 1->1 Split: No split if the split result is equal to the actual delivery

SAP Application Exercise

* At first, an order for 10 pieces of "Test1" material is entered into the system.

* The following delivery document is created for the material and quantity with the order reference.

* To split the delivery document, go to the "VLSP" transaction code. "Delivery document" and "Split profile" are entered and the "Run" button is pressed.

The requested amount is entered in the "Split amount UoM" field on the screen. Additionally, the document that will result from split can be simulated with the "Split Simulation" function without storing the document directly.

After the document is simulated, the "Save Split" function is executed to replace the document.

A different delivery was created from the original delivery.

The original delivery is re-entered, and one delivery has been created for 1 qty.

Different delivery documents created at the moment the order document flow is entered can be viewed.

Transportation Document Assignment

If the 'Result in Shpmt' and 'IncRmdrInShpmt' fields in the separation profile are selected, and the delivery document to be separated is within the transportation document, the new document is also assigned to the transportation document.

The sample delivery document (80000302) has been assigned to the transportation document (5000000641) as shown below.

When a single separation process is performed using the 'VLSP' transaction code, the following documents are created.

As a result of the dispatched delivery separation, a single delivery with number '80000390' is created. The original delivery with number '80000320' is revised into 6 units.

As a result of the transportation assignment adjustment, the document created from the separation will also be included in the transportation document.

entry.title: Re: Decommissioning Cockpit - Meaning of "is referenced"
entry.content:
Hello,

The attribute "is referenced" indicates whether custom code is used in other parts of the system. If a code is referenced, it means it is being used or called by other programs or functionalities. This information is important during decommissioning to avoid impacting other parts of the system when removing or modifying the code.

For more information, see the content below:

  • KBA 3383256 Custom Code - References: Attribute "is Referenced" is not filled in CCLM - SAP Solution Manager 7.2.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Query Browser interface - save as a Tile
entry.content:
Hi Abhishek,

This might be a little late, but the solution offered in this post helped me.

Solved: How to set Custom Analytical Query as App Tile and... - SAP Community

[copying answer from the post linked above]

Try to create a query, and then copy the query name, go to a new catalog, create a static tile and disable the Semantic object navigation, and instead, in the Target URL field, provide the url in the following format:

#AnalyticQuery-analyze?XQUERY=2C<SQL Name of CDS>/<query name>

Save the tile, assign it to a Group in the designer, and then share to the other users.

entry.title: Re: Query Browser interface - save as a Tile

entry.title: Can Rapid FP&A be used with S/4HANA 2022 On-Premise, and how to configure Standard CDS?
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am currently working on implementing Rapid FP&A in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) with S/4HANA 2022 On-Premise. The documentation I’ve come across primarily mentions SAP S/4HANA Public & Private Cloud Editions, but I haven’t found clear guidance for S/4HANA On-Premise.

My Key Questions:
Can we use the Rapid FP&A Suite with the 2022 On-Premise version of S/4HANA?
The documentation and resources mention connectivity primarily for cloud editions, and I would like confirmation on whether the suite is compatible with on-premise.
Steps to Configure Standard CDS Views for On-Premise:
I am trying to configure the Standard CDS (for example, GLAccountBalanceFlowQuery found in the SAP_FI_FPA_IM_S4FinancialPlan model) in our on-premise S/4HANA environment.
We are facing issues, such as permissions errors and inability to refresh queries, which seem to be cloud-related setups. However, we are on S/4HANA On-Premise 2022.

I've looked for existing Standard CDS views related to FP&A in our on-premise system, but I haven't found relevant resources specific to on-premise.
Encountered a permission issue with GLAccountBalanceFlowQuery during refresh attempts (attached error).


Can the community help clarify whether the Rapid FP&A suite can be used with S/4HANA On-Premise?
If so, could you guide me through the steps required to configure the relevant Standard CDS views (or build custom ones) for FP&A models on-premise?
Any detailed steps, documentation, or guidance would be highly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: Update S-user's E-mail Addresses.
entry.content:
Hello,

Regarding the request of updating the email address of a S-user, it is mandatory to contact an active super admin of the company to proceed with the update, accordingly to the steps on KBA 1271545 - How to update SAP for Me and S-user ID settings (phone number, e-mail address, time zone, language, etc) - SAP for Me.

Also, a Super Administrator has to be contacted to add the new domain of the company, insuring the security and the validity of the user, as per on KBA 3025172 - How to add/remove email domains - SAP for Me.

Thank you,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Create and change tiles are not available for sales i...
entry.content:
Hello,

There are no dedicated tiles for those apps. You can navigate to those apps by one of those paths:

Through the respective Manage app: 

  • Manage Sales Quotations or Manage Sales Quotations - version 2 for the apps VA21 and VA22.
  • Manage Sales Inquiries for the apps VA11 and VA12.

Through the WebGUI app for display.

  1. Access the display app: Display Sales Inquiries (VA13) or Display Sales Quotations (VA23).
  2. Go to Menu -> Sales Document.
  3. Select Create or Change.

Best regards.

entry.title: Enqueue Read Validation
entry.content:
Hi,

We are having a Z table containing credit limit wrt customer, business, payment terms and an Unique Id. Whenever this Unique Id is attached to any sale order @ Sales order creation or Sales order change, the payment terms gets attached with the order.

Also, the credit limit gets consumed accordingly wrt current order value and the orders in which this Unique Id is attached. For correct credit limit consumption, we have enabled lock/Enqueue for the orders wrt Unique Id(thats like, users cannot simultaneously save multiple orders with same Unique Id, it happens one by one)

Question:

1. Business says, we are unable to process orders simultaneously, they want users to save orders without enqueue, still want correct credit limit consumption - Is there anyway out for this?

2. Can we do like this - While doing Enqueue while creating or changing order containing Unique Id, can we pass capping credit limits for each simultaneous order save in SM12 and read the same in "USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT" and use below code to stop further processing of order -- Will there be a sales order number skip as SO number would get generated in this exit?

MESSAGE Sxxx. "to tell the user what's the problem
PERFORM FOLGE_GLEICHSETZEN(SAPLV00F).
FCODE = 'ENT1'.
SET SCREEN SYST-DYNNR.
LEAVE SCREEN.

While we do understand, validations have to happen in USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE, but we are not getting the exact SO value in USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE, as freight and tax computation happens only in "USEREXIT_CHECK_VBAK" or in "USEREXIT_CHECK_VBAP".

Waiting for your suggestions

With Regards,
Jagan

entry.title: SAP Standard Content Vs. Custom-built Content for End-User Adoption in SAP Implementation Programs
entry.content:
I have been a part of various SAP Implementation programs to lead the efforts for End-User Adoption. During various customer engagements, I have observed the SAP Standard Content, plus have anchored teams to build plentiful Custom Content for End User trainings.

In this blog, I will share my findings on whether we can use SAP Standard Content alone for End-User training / adoption.

SAP provides the standard content through various ways, such as SAP Learning Hub, SAP Enable Now, and Help Documentation. Remember that SAP standard content provides good information; however, using the standard content alone for End-User Training is generally not sufficient. 

Standard Content is generic in nature

Standard content is typically the generic content provided by SAP. This standard content is designed for a broad industry; it intends to be flexible to cover a wide range of industries and business functions.

The standard SAP content is drafted with model company, model configurations, best practices, and out-of-the-box functionalities. Some standard content does happen to be the cross-industry content, for example, the standard content like Finance, Procurement, HR, and Supply Chain applies across industries.

While this standard content from SAP is definitely good, but it is not tailored to specific industry or organization. This standard content does not fully address the organization-specific processes, configurations, custom workflows, business rules, policies, local regulations, legacy system integrations, etc. made during the implementation for the organization.

Standard Content lacks the implementation story / context

End Users typically prefer practical & role-based training, with real organizational processes. Additionally, while learning about process executions in the new system, the End Users also wish to get the following  insights integrated with the process explanations, precisely & realistically:

  • SAP integrations with their organization's legacy systems
  • Business rules & SOPs
  • What’s changing information
  • Cross-role collaboration
  • Inter-departmental connections
  • Organization-specific settings & controls configured in the system
  • Localizations
  • ...and more

Standard SAP documentation assumes SAP-centric environment, and generally does not cover the above-mentioned areas for the organization. In short, the standard content does not align directly with organization-specific user roles, integrations, policies, plus the overall implementation story & context; thus, making it difficult for End Users to apply the standard content in daily tasks. As a result, this leads us to the development of custom training content for End Users.

Standard Content does not reflect organization-specific workflows

In the initial phase of the implementation program, Functional / Technical Consultants gather requirements for the implementation, do Fit-Gap analysis, and then define multiple TO-BE business workflows, specific to the client's organization. These TO-BE workflows (typically swim-lane diagrams) cover how the organization's real business processes will be facilitated through the new system, encompassing various business roles, business rules, policies, legacy systems, and inter-departmental connections. Every such workflow tells a story from the organization’s perspective.

Now, here remember that these TO-BE workflows will play a crucial role for the following two scenarios:

  • First, Application Development Team / System Configuration Team need to refer to these workflows to develop & configure the new system to make it behave, exactly as expected by the client.
  • Second, Business Users / End Users must comprehend these organization-specific TO-BE workflows in a simplified way to know the new system's behavior and their interactions with the new system.

Here, the standard content from SAP will help the users understand the processes in general, but not in the context of the client's organization. As a result, this leads us to the development of custom content, explaining these TO-BE workflows in a simplified way (e.g., happy flows, alternate flows, exceptions, and other variables).

Standard Content does not reflect organization-specific system settings & controls

Here, let's assume that the SAP system is implemented with out-of-the box functionalities (with no customizations) for a client's organization. Now, even with out-of-the-box functionalities, the Implementation Team need to modify / customize certain aspects during the system set up / configuration, for example:

  • Configuring organization-specific product / material codes, catalogs, pricing conditions, roles-security constraints, order types, localization settings, and so on.
  • Enabling and disabling various controls in the system to make the system behave as per organization's requirements.

Now, when it comes to training, End Users prefer to learn the new system along with all these specifics configured / set in the new system. The standard content will not reflect the client-specific settings & controls, as it is drafted based on model company, configurations, settings, and SAP-centric environment. As a result, this leads us to the development of custom content to make it highly specific to the End Users.

Documentation Vs. Learning Experience

SAP’s standard content can be considered more like good product documentation / manual, explaining SAP’s product features, functional capabilities, and process overviews with probable business scenarios. This standard content is good reading material as a great starting point. However, we cannot achieve the required training / learning experience with the standard content.

Learning experience is typically driven by clear learning objectives tailored to user needs; practical & organization-specific context for better relevance; interactive elements (quizzes, simulations, scenario-based learning, micro-learning, real-life examples, interactive exercises, quiz, etc.) for better user engagement and retention, and so on.

Hence, if the goal is skill-building, knowledge retention, and behavioral change in End Users, then the carefully custom-built & instructionally-sound training is the best approach.

Consistency in Future Onboarding

When an organization undertakes a large-scale implementation and invests in Training, the organization also thinks about the future returns on the investment. For example, the organization wants to utilize the training materials for future onboardings of new hires, or use the training materials when employees leave or switch roles, etc.

  • Having custom-built training content with the organization-specific context & details ensures that every new hire receives the same level of knowledge & skills. If the organization simply relies on the standard content, then it will result in longer & inconsistent onboarding.
  • Having well-documented custom training materials help to maintain knowledge continuity as employees leave or change roles.

Different User Roles Using SAP in Unique Ways

In an organization, there will be different roles, who will interact with the new SAP system in unique ways, and will also have different comfort levels to use the modern technology / systems. For example, there will be roles, such as Warehouse Workers, Finance Teams, HR Staff, Procurement Specialists, etc. 

Now, when the transformation is planned to happen in a big way (in just a few months) from old to modern systems, these roles will have their own apprehensions & reservations about how they will transition to the new world suddenly.

Here, to safely & carefully take these users to the new world and maintain the momentum for business operations in the new system from day 1 after the system go-live, custom training content needs to be built. This custom content will precisely & realistically reflect the daily workflows plus scenarios and system navigation for each role. 

Some user groups may learn to use the new system just based on the standard content; however, we cannot generalize this for all user groups, specifically when the transformation happens at a large scale, and the organization's business operations & productivity will be at stake from day 1 after the go-live. In short, we cannot simply rely on the standard SAP content & generic system navigation, and expect that all user roles will transition confidently to the new world. 

Conclusion

Training Team can effectively use SAP’s standard content, user guides, and process documentation as a base to build the initial understanding quickly, and then proceed to fast-track the development of custom training content, in line with client-specific workflows, business rules, policies, legacy integrations, what’s changing information, system settings, cross-role collaboration, and so on.

On the other side, as part of the flipped learning approach, organizations can use the SAP’s standard content as a great starting point to familiarize their employees with the new SAP product / system, its features, process overviews, functional capabilities, etc. and then formally execute the custom-built training content sessions. This ensures that end-users understand and effectively use the new system in their daily work.

entry.title: Re: Integration of QR Code Scanning in SAP Module Pool Using HTML Service
entry.content:
It was difficult to understand the logic, here's my description of what I understand (I hope it's clear):

First, the ABAP screen is displayed:

The user presses a button "Scan the Material QR" on the ABAP screen, which adds an entry in the table zpp_tab_sess_id and opens a Web page in the Web browser, starting the ABAP ICF service via GET method. This Web page allows scanning via the Web utility Html5QRCodeScanner:

<screenshot to add>

The user does the scan via the Web page which then triggers the ABAP ICF service via POST method, the scanned QR code text is stored back into the table zpp_tab_sess_id.

In the ABAP screen, some action (or an automatic scan till the scanned QR code in table zpp_tab_sess_id is filled?) reads the scanned QR code from the table zpp_tab_sess_id and displays it in the ABAP screen.

entry.title: Process Goverannce Issues: One with "Modeler" role and another where the Process Workflow Stops
entry.content:
I'm creating a workflow for process review and approval and am having 2 issues.

Overview:

The workflow does the following:

  • 3 simultaneous tasks- change process state to "pending QA", email assigned task owners, create multi-assignee task to QA review a process and either provide feedback and reject or approve.
  • Upon approval of QA, 3 more simultaneous tasks- change process state to "pending Performance metrics", email modeler to notify of a task, and assign the modeler a task asking them to confirm they've added performance metrics by selecting "yes" OR  state that they don't need any and select "no"
  • If they select "no" an email and task are generated that are assigned to the Assurance group.
  • Assurance group can either allow the process to move on without metrics, or demand that the modeler add metrics.  WHEN THIS HAPPENS, the workflow emails the modeler to let them know, and RECREATES the task for them to add metrics.

The task recreation to add metrics works fine, but once they add the metrics and select "yes"- the case just ends- it does not continue on to the Assurance team to review the metrics as is defined in the workflow.

NOTE:  If the modeler confirms that the metrics are generated in the first pass at this, then the workflow continues as expected.

Why does the workflow not continue after the modeler completes the performance metrics task the 2nd time?

Issue # 2:  I have used "Modeler" to assign tasks to the modeler and have used it to select who to send emails to, but the "modeler" role that's available to select in Process Governance workflows does not seem to get populated and the task remains unassigned and the email does not fire.  Why?

Oh- one more issue:  Since Modeler didn't work, I assigned the task to "Case / Creator" instead.  That works fine, but the task shows the little dot above it and states that the task is not assigned.  Why does this happen?

entry.title: Prevent Trasnsfer from MSEG-SGTXT to Accounting Document during MIGO

When the subsequent Accounting Document is generated, this value is copied through and prevents G/L account summarization during FI posting.

The ask to is to simply NOT transfer the value from the Material Document (MSEG-SGTXT) to the Accounting Document while keeping the value in the Material Document intact.
